title: Nursery Rhyme. CCLXVII. Gaffers And Gammers.
content:
[The following is part of a comic song called 'Success to the Whistle and Wig,' intended to be sung in rotation by the members of a club.]

        There was an old woman had three sons,
        Jerry, and James, and John:
        Jerry was hung, James was drowned,
        John was lost and never was found,
        And there was an end of the three sons,
        Jerry, and James, and John!
meaning:
Unknown's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Nursery Rhyme. CCLXVII. Gaffers And Gammers."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
